description: Add a compact, well-ordered filter UI to the Bill Statements page. Filters should include search by name, status (active/inactive), and a date range picker for statement date or due date. The design should match the modernized expense filter style (clean, no neo-brutalist).
steps: Create a new component src/components/bill-statements/bill-statements-filters.tsx. It should accept props: billStatements (the full array), onFilteredChange (callback that returns the filtered array). The component manages local filter state and applies client-side filtering (since the bill statements API does not support server-side filtering - it returns all statements).

The filter UI should be a single compact row (responsive grid) with: (1) A search input filtering by name (with Search icon, same pattern as expenses filter), (2) A status select with options: 'All status', 'Active', 'Inactive', (3) A date filter select to choose which date field to filter: 'Statement date' or 'Due date', (4) A date range picker (same modern style with presets as the expenses date picker). Include a Clear button that resets all filters. Filters should apply immediately on change (no Apply button needed since this is client-side). Use standard shadcn/ui component styling (thin borders, no neo-brutalist shadows).
Export the new component from src/components/bill-statements/index.ts.

Integrate the filter into src/app/(dashboard)/bill-statements/page.tsx: Import BillStatementsFilters. Add state for filtered bill statements. Place the filter component between the stats cards and the table. Pass the full billStatements array to the filter, and use the filtered result for both the stats cards and the table. The stats cards should update to reflect filtered counts.
Ensure all labels use Sentence case: 'Search bill statements...', 'All status', 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Statement date', 'Due date'.
acceptance_criteria: The bill statements page shows filter controls above the table
Filters include: text search by name, status dropdown (All/Active/Inactive), date field selector, date range picker with presets
Filtering is client-side and updates the displayed table and stats cards in real-time
Filter UI uses clean modern styling consistent with the modernized expenses filter (no neo-brutalist patterns)
verification: Run pnpm build to confirm the project compiles successfully
Run pnpm lint to confirm no linting errors
